texto: Just because you don't believe in something doesn't mean its not real
explicacao: Citação de McCormick Templeman sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Just because you don't believe in something doesn't mean its not real” articula vida, believe, because em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como vida.

texto: Turning, she looked out at the icy landscape, the soaring trees, branches dark and bitten blue with frost. And for a moment, she had something like a premonition, a feeling that something terrible was watching her, something hungry and sick. She could nearly hear it out there, panting between the trees, its breath ragged and spoiled.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a McCormick Templeman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Turning, she looked out at the icy landscape, the soaring trees, branches dark and bitten blue wi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, fear, hunted pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e hunted.

frase_original: Turning, she looked out at the icy landscape, the soaring trees, branches dark and bitten blue with frost. And for a moment, she had something like a premonition, a feeling that something terrible was watching her, something hungry and sick. She could nearly hear it out there, panting between the trees, its breath ragged and spoiled.
